lecture_context.lecture_title: What We See and What We Value: AI with the Human Perspective

unit.name: From objects to relationships, language, and dynamic scenes
unit.description: Expands visual intelligence beyond labels to scene graphs, captioning, and dynamic multi-actor understanding, while stressing remaining open problems.
unit.summary: After object recognition, the lecture argues that the same objects can imply very different situations depending on their relationships, motivating structured representations such as scene graphs with objects as nodes and relations as edges [ts=914s] [ts=991s]. Fei-Fei Li highlights work on Visual Genome and zero-shot relational reasoning, showing how common relations like 'person riding horse' can support unusual combinations like 'horse wearing hat' through compositional structure [ts=1030s] [ts=1074s]. She then traces the move to image captioning and dense captioning with CNN+LSTM models, notes that multimodal LLMs have advanced this further, and closes by emphasizing that dynamic multi-actor activity understanding remains unsolved and crucial for everyday robotics [ts=1140s] [ts=1271s].

key_points.text: Visual intelligence must encode relationships between objects, not just object labels.
A scene graph represents objects as entity nodes and relationships as connectivity or attributes.
Compositional scene graphs enable zero-shot recognition of unusual relationships such as 'horse wearing hat'.
Image captioning and dense captioning emerged from combining CNNs with LSTMs.
Dynamic multi-object, multi-actor activity understanding is still unsolved and important for robotics.

kp_catalog.name: Scene graphs and relational perception
kp_catalog.description: Scene understanding requires explicit representations of objects, attributes, and relations rather than isolated labels.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because scene graphs and relational perception clarifies scene understanding requires explicit representations of objects, attributes, and relations rather than isolated labels, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Image captioning and multimodal scene description
kp_catalog.description: Vision systems can map images to natural language descriptions, first with CNN+LSTM pipelines and later with multimodal LLMs.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because image captioning and multimodal scene description clarifies vision systems can map images to natural language descriptions, first with CNN+LSTM pipelines and later with multimodal LLMs, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Dynamic multi-actor activity understanding
kp_catalog.description: Understanding who is doing what and what happens next in videos remains an open problem beyond static scene recognition.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because dynamic multi-actor activity understanding synthesizes understanding who is doing what and what happens next in videos remains an open problem beyond static scene recognition into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.

kp_catalog.name: Deep learning resurgence from data, compute, and scale
kp_catalog.description: Why modern neural NLP depends on a set of training and modeling ideas that made deep networks workable after years of difficulty. Large-scale datasets, GPU computation, and neural architectures converged to unlock modern deep learning for vision.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because deep learning resurgence from data, compute, and scale formalizes how why modern neural NLP depends on a set of training and modeling ideas that made deep networks workable after years of difficulty, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.
